turns.agentText:
I’m treating this as a squad-comp request, and the first gap is mode and scale because a valid comp depends on both. I’m checking which officer tools are available so I can ground the recommendation in the current meta and render the comp correctly.

I’ve got the officer tools, and this needs one clarification before I build anything because comp shape changes completely by mode and headcount. I’m pulling any saved guild comp preferences now so I can anchor the recommendation once you pick the format.

A usable comp depends on mode and squad size.

If you mean `WvW`, tell me which scale and I’ll give you a proper subgrouped roster with role coverage checked:
1. `Roaming` 1-5
2. `Havoc` 5-10
3. `Zerg` 15-50

If you meant `PvE` instead, say `raid`, `fractal`, or `open world`.

A 30-35 player WvW comp wants 6 to 7 subgroups built around per-subgroup support coverage, not a fixed 10-man PvE frame. I’m pulling the current WvW role guidance, the wiki’s 5-target subgroup rule, and any saved guild playbook before I draft the roster.

The first wiki lookup was too broad, so I’m narrowing it to the squad/subgroup mechanic and the named WvW support duties. After that I’ll validate a 30-35 roster as 7 subgroups of 5 before I present it.

I’ve got the subgroup rule and the broad zerg ranges; now I’m fetching exact current WvW build sources for the support spine and the DPS packages so I can attach the right roles to each slot instead of freehanding from memory.

The 34-player draft passed comp validation with no subgroup or squad-wide coverage errors. I’m rendering it as a fixed 7-party shell and I’ll call out the one deliberate tradeoff: it drops classic Scrapper mobility for heavier boon-strip and sustain.

This 34-player WvW zerg shell gives every subgroup a stability/sustain anchor and passed `comp_check` with no coverage errors.

{{figure}}

I built it as seven 5-player subgroups because the GW2 Wiki’s [Boon](https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Boon) page says boon distribution prioritizes your own party/subgroup first, which is the reason organized WvW packs players into 5s around allied-target caps. For the role spine, I used Guild Order’s updated WvW squad guide ([guildorder.com](https://guildorder.com/games/gw2/guides/wvw-squad-leadership)), GW2Mists’ [Support Firebrand](https://gw2mists.com/en/builds/guardian/support-firebrand), MetaBattle’s [Support Druid](https://metabattle.com/wiki/Build:Druid_-_Support_Druid), and MetaBattle’s current [WvW build index](https://metabattle.com/wiki/WvW), which lists the rest of these zerg builds.

What this comp is doing:
- Each subgroup has `Primary Support` from `Firebrand` or `Luminary`, plus `Secondary Support` from `Druid` or `Specter`.
- Squad-wide strip/control is covered by `Necromancer` core boon-strip slots, one `Untamed`, and the two `Harbinger` groups.
- Pure conversion damage is mostly `Reaper`, with `Tempest`, `Amalgam`, and `Berserker` as pressure flexes.

Deliberate tradeoff:
- It doubles up on primary and secondary supports on purpose.
- It is missing the classic `Scrapper` mobility/revive package from the older Firebrand/Scrapper shell, so you gain strip and sustain but lose some superspeed and stomp utility. If your group values movement more than extra strip, swap `1 Tempest -> 1 Scrapper` and `1 Berserker -> 1 Scrapper`.

If you’re closer to `30` than `35`, keep all seven support pairs and cut damage first: bench `1 Amalgam`, `1 Berserker`, `1 Tempest`, and `1 Reaper`.

builds.note: Primary support anchor: subgroup stability, healing, and boon floor.
Primary support variant for two parties; same stability anchor role with a different guardian package.
Secondary support: cleanse, sustain, and recovery layer for most groups.
Secondary support flex for one party; extra sustain and cleanse coverage.
Tertiary support: extra sustain and boon support in the commander core groups.
Tertiary support with boon corrupt pressure; use on call-driven strip groups.
Core boon-strip DPS slot; helps crack enemy stability and boons on engage.
Extra boon-strip / control flex slot for one spike group.
Main pure-DPS packet for melee conversion after strip and CC windows.
Backline power pressure for cleave and ranged follow-through.
Melee pressure flex where you want more front-loaded hammer damage.
Extra melee spike DPS for groups that already have enough strip.

turns.userText: hmm id like a troub per line, 1 necro per line too.
turns.agentText:
A troub-per-line and necro-per-line shell is workable at 35, but it forces the comp into a very support-heavy shape with only one true flex/DPS slot per subgroup. I’m rebalancing around that constraint now and checking it before I show it.

The revised 35 passed cleanly. I’m rendering it as the strict version of your request and I’ll note the cost clearly: you’re doubling tertiary support in every subgroup, so your pressure ceiling is lower than the earlier shell.

A strict `troub per line` and `necro per line` 35-man comp works, and this version passed `comp_check` with no errors.

{{figure}}

The tradeoff is straightforward: you are doubling up on tertiary-support / utility coverage in every subgroup, so the comp is safer and more stable, but it has a lower damage ceiling than the previous shell. That fits if your group wins on uptime, strips, and clean engages rather than raw spike.

Role-wise, this stays inside current WvW zerg guidance instead of importing a PvE frame: per Snowcrows’ WvW roles guide ([snowcrows.com](https://snowcrows.com/guides/wvw/wvw-basics-understanding-roles)) and Guild Order’s squad-leadership guide ([guildorder.com](https://guildorder.com/games/gw2/guides/wvw-squad-leadership)), each subgroup still has a primary support anchor, sustain/cleanse, and either boon-strip or corrupt pressure. I kept it in 7 subgroups of 5 because the GW2 Wiki’s [Boon](https://wiki.guildwars2.com/wiki/Boon) page says subgroup allies get boon priority first, which is why organized WvW comping centers on 5-player lines.

If you want, I can do the next pass in one of two directions:
- `more damage`: keep troub per line, but make every necro slot `Core Necro` or `Reaper` and trim Harbinger support.
- `more utility`: keep this exact shell, but swap the `Amalgam` and `Berserker` flex slots into `Scrapper` or `Untamed`.

data.title: WvW Zerg — 35
data.subtitle: 7 subgroups · troub per line · necro per line · validated for 5-target subgroup coverage

builds.note: Primary support anchor for five groups: stability, healing, and baseline boon coverage.
Primary support anchor for two groups; same stability job in a different guardian package.
Secondary support backbone: cleanse, sustain, and recovery layer.
Secondary support flex for one party; extra sustain and cleanse coverage.
One per line as requested; tertiary support and party sustain in every subgroup.
Core Necro boon-strip slot in five groups to help break enemy boons and stability.
Necromancer-line support variant in two groups; keeps the necro-per-line rule while adding corrupt pressure.
Pure conversion DPS in groups that already have clean strip coverage.
Backline pressure and cleave from the damage slot.
Melee hammer pressure flex.
Extra melee spike flex.
